Comparative & epitranscriptomic regulation

zemke-2023-conserved-and-divergent-gene-regulatory (2023) — cross-species single-cell multiomics: transposable-element-driven cis-regulatory divergence shapes neocortex evolution (Science)
sun-2023-crosstalk-between-rna-m6a (2023) — m6A reader YTHDC2 → TET1 keeps LTR7/HERV-H demethylated, restraining neural differentiation (Nat Genet) · human
li-2023-super-enhancer-rna-m6a-promotes-local (2023) — super-enhancer-RNA m6A opens local chromatin to drive oncogene transcription in pancreatic cancer (Nat Genet) · human

Concept map

Elements — cis-regulatory-elements · massively-parallel-reporter-assay
Grammar — cis-regulatory-grammar · chromatin-accessibility
Wiring — chromatin-loops · topologically-associating-domains · genome-architecture
Variation — noncoding-variants · dna-methylation

Current synthesis

From cataloguing to grammar to wiring. The topic runs from finding enhancers
(p300 ChIP-seq, eRNA, ENCODE cCREs) to reading their sequence grammar
(cis-regulatory-grammar) to connecting elements to genes — where the emerging
picture is broad enhancer–promoter compatibility (bergman-2022-compatibility-rules-of-human-enhancer)
that becomes more instructive and physically closer as cells differentiate
(pollex-2024-enhancer-promoter-interactions-become-more, chen-2024-increased-enhancer-promoter-interactions-during).
Regulatory variation is coordinated and mostly noncoding. Sequence variants
perturb TF binding, chromatin, and transcription in a haplotype-coordinated way with
TFs as primary drivers (kilpinen-2013-coordinated-effects-of-sequence-variation);
interpreting such noncoding-variants is the throughline into disease genetics.
Regulation evolves and is layered. cis-regulatory divergence (often
transposable-element-driven) underlies neocortex evolution
(zemke-2023-conserved-and-divergent-gene-regulatory), and an epitranscriptomic
(m6A) layer tunes enhancer/TE activity and chromatin accessibility
(sun-2023-crosstalk-between-rna-m6a, li-2023-super-enhancer-rna-m6a-promotes-local).

Open questions

What is the true “grammar” mapping enhancer sequence → activity → target gene, and can models learn it? (cross-topic: artificial-intelligence sequence models)
How instructive vs permissive are E–P contacts in mammals, causally? (chromatin-loops)
Which noncoding regulatory variants are causal for disease, and through which cell type and stage? (noncoding-variants, brain-development-neurogenomics)


Cross-topic: predictive sequence-to-function models (artificial-intelligence),
3D folding (3d-genome), and regulatory disease variants
(brain-development-neurogenomics, splicing) all build on the elements and
grammar catalogued here.

wiki/topics/splicing.title: splicing
wiki/topics/splicing.content:
Splicing
Topic hub for pre-mRNA splicing and alternative splicing — the removal
of introns from precursor mRNA by the spliceosome and its regulation, which
together form a central layer of eukaryotic gene regulation. The wiki’s deepest
topic: this page curates the sources (grouped below), the concept map, and the
current synthesis.
Why it matters
Splicing converts intron-containing “mosaic” genes into mature mRNA via a
two-step transesterification reaction. alternative-splicing — the regulated
inclusion/exclusion of exons — lets one gene produce many isoforms, expanding
the proteome and underpinning organismal complexity, development, disease, and
therapy (see editorial-2025-rna-splicing-a-central-layer).
Sources (83)
Foundational biology & reviews

Current synthesis (across the 80 sources)

The brain is splicing’s deep end. A large cluster maps
[[splicing-and-neurodevelopment|splicing across brain development, aging and
disease]]: neuron-specific regulators (PTBP/Rbfox/Nova/SRRM4) drive timed exon
programs (su-2018-alternative-splicing-in-neurogenesis-and,
weyn-vanhentenryck-2018-precise-temporal-regulation-of-alternative); neurons
diverge from the splicing-code more than glia
(michielsen-2026-decoding-exon-inclusion-in); cross-ancestry developmental
e/sQTL atlases show isoform/splicing regulation mediates the most
neuropsychiatric GWAS heritability (wen-2024-cross-ancestry-atlas-of-gene-isoform,
walker-2019-genetic-control-of-expression-and,
li-2018-integrative-functional-genomic-analysis); and disease links span
autism (parikshak-2016-genome-wide-changes-in-lncrna), Huntington’s
(nguyen-2025-aberrant-splicing-in-huntingtons-disease) and druggable
poison-exons (pigini-2026-poison-exon-splicing-in,
neil-2025-poison-exons-tuning-rna-splicing). rna-editing is tracked as a
sibling RNA-processing layer.
Long reads are resolving isoforms directly. A 2024–2026 wave of
long-read-rna-seq moves splicing from event-inference to full-length isoform
observation — single-cell/spatial (longcell), cis/trans separation by phasing
(isolaser, allele-specific-splicing), cell-type isoform atlases powering
sQTL discovery (isomiga, aida), direct RNA-seq reading
modifications and splicing together (molm13-drna-seq), and exposing
ancestry bias in reference annotation (poder-annotation, a
data-quality-and-bias instance). Clinically, splicing programs define and
treat disease subtypes (oncosplice, leukemia).
The machine is settled; the regulation is the frontier. The spliceosome
is a ~4.8 MDa dynamic RNP of five snRNPs whose catalytic core is RNA — a
ribozyme related to group II introns (sharp-1994-split-genes-and-rna-splicing,
lee-2015-mechanisms-and-regulation-of-alternative); cryo-EM resolved most
states (rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing). Open questions are
about control, not chemistry.
AS is nearly universal. Founding mRNA-seq surveys put AS at ~92–95% of
multiexon human genes (wang-2008-alternative-isoform-regulation-in-human,
pan-2008-deep-surveying-of-alternative-splicing); it expands the proteome
(nilsen-2010-expansion-of-the-eukaryotic-proteome) and is read out per-event
as PSI.
A position-dependent, combinatorial code. Weak metazoan splice-sites +
splicing-regulatory-elements read by splicing-factors/rna-binding-proteins
in a context-dependent way (fu-2014-context-dependent-control-of-alternative-splicing,
witten-2011-understanding-splicing-regulation-through-rna,
wang-2008-splicing-regulation-from-a-parts); RBP concentration itself gates
outcomes (begg-2020-concentration-dependent-splicing-is-enabled). Modeling
this splicing-code went from PWMs to deep learning (SpliceAI, darts,
capitanchik-2024-from-computational-models-of-the).
A mature measurement ecosystem. Splicing is quantified two ways —
isoform-based (cufflinks, kallisto, suppa) and event-based
(rmats/rmats-turbo, miso, dexseq, leafcutter, majiq-v2,
psi-sigma, splicetrap) — over a decade of rna-seq tooling
(sousa-luis-2026-tools-and-tactics-for-studying,
conesa-2016-survey-of-best-practices,
stark-2019-rna-sequencing-the-teenage-years). Tools disagree, so orthogonal
validation matters.
Genetics connects sequence to splicing to disease. Population RNA-seq maps
sQTLs and allele-specific splicing
(park-2018-the-expanding-landscape-of-alternative,
zhang-2020-regional-variation-of-splicing-qtls,
demirdjian-2020-detecting-allele-specific-alternative-splicing); RBP
binding/function atlases (van-nostrand-2020-a-large-scale-binding-and-functional)
and isoform/proteome catalogues
(mironov-2021-an-extended-catalogue-of-tandem,
lau-2019-splice-junction-based-mapping-of) ground the regulatory logic.
Timing is itself regulatory. Splicing is largely co-transcriptional, but a
large fraction is post-transcriptional
(choquet-2025-the-regulation-and-function-of,
shine-2024-co-transcriptional-gene-regulation-in), and intron-retention
is a widespread regulatory event.
Mechanism → medicine. Splicing-factor mutations drive cancer and genetic
disease; the first splicing therapies are approved (nusinersen, risdiplam,
eteplirsen) — see scotti-2015-rna-mis-splicing-in-disease,
splicing-therapeutics, splicing-and-disease.

Open questions

What triggers the co- vs post-transcriptional timing switch? (choquet-2025-the-regulation-and-function-of)
How much alternative splicing is functional vs noise? (marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative / wright-2022-alternative-splicing-as-a-source)
Can splicing-code models become interpretable, tissue-specific, and predict activating variants? (capitanchik-2024-from-computational-models-of-the)
Why do splicing-quantification tools disagree, and which is right when? (sousa-luis-2026-tools-and-tactics-for-studying)
